text_x: Highlight Clip CFTC Chair: Paving the Way for On-Chain Markets Like Hyperliquid to Come Onshore On June 15, 2026, CFTC Chairman Mike Selig discussed the regulatory pathway for bringing decentralized perpetual contract platforms like Hyperliquid to the United States on the Bankless podcast. He stated that blockchain technology and 24/7 trading models are transforming market operations, and regulators should lean into the future rather than rigidly applying outdated 1934 exchange rules. Selig believes that while on-chain mechanisms like auto-deleveraging introduce new considerations, the CFTC is actively working to tailor regulations for these new products and platforms. He expressed that the core objective is to create a compliant path that keeps the new frontier of finance building and flourishing within the US, provided that investor funds are protected and adequate disclosures are ensured. — link

text_x: WSJ: Charles Schwab, One of America's Largest Brokerages, Plans Prediction-Market-Like Binary Options Offering According to The Wall Street Journal, Charles Schwab, one of the largest brokerage firms in the United States, is working with Cboe Global Markets to launch all-or-nothing binary options that allow customers to place yes-or-no wagers on the performance of the S&P 500. While the contracts differ structurally from event contracts offered by platforms such as Kalshi and Polymarket, they function in a similar way, paying a fixed cash settlement or nothing depending on whether the index closes above or below a target level. The products are expected to roll out in the coming months. — link

text_x: Post Agent Companies: Eyes on the End Game > The end game is not building more capabilities and applications for computers. > The end game is to bet on cheaper, faster, local and open source models that make intelligence free. > The next wave of opportunities revolves around using computers, not just teaching them. > Directing and deploying 24/7 intelligence to do more work (raw volume), cheaper work (cost), better work (quality), and the long-tail of tasks that never made economic sense to do before. > The post-agent business assumes software and cognitive/computer-based labor is going to zero. > Therefore, the winning strategy is to capture what remains scarce: context, attention, trust, brand, and network effects that create durable differentiation. text_x: Decrypt: Hackers Exploit Steam's Popular Wallpaper Engine to Spread Crypto-Stealing Malware Attackers are abusing Steam Workshop through Wallpaper Engine, one of Steam's most popular live-wallpaper apps, to distribute malicious downloads disguised as animated wallpapers, many using anime-style female characters as covers and some reaching thousands or tens of thousands of installs. Kaspersky said the malicious wallpapers can steal Steam credentials, hijack account sessions and deliver infostealers such as Lumma and Vidar, targeting browser data, login credentials and crypto wallet information. — link

text_x: Oman, Arabian Peninsula's Second-Largest Country, Launches National Bitcoin Mining Pool Oman's Ministry of Transport, Communications and Information Technology, together with Frontier Technologies, launched Omanhash, the country's national Bitcoin mining pool. Under Oman's regulatory framework, Omanhash will serve as the only official pool for licensed crypto mining companies, with all licensed miners required to connect to it. The pool is expected to consolidate about 10 EH/s of hashrate in its initial phase. Oman has invested more than $700 million in mining and data-center infrastructure since 2022. — link

text_x: Saylor is in the money on STRC and its simpler than you think STRC is a coin-margined Bitcoin perp equivalent, where MicroStrategy uses its BTC holdings as collateral to maintain a leveraged long position on Bitcoin with predictable funding-like dividend payments Frames MSTR as a Bitcoin trading hedge fund that speculates via leverage, with STRC holders providing the funding; the recent STRC price drop below $100 reflects reduced demand from lower BTC prices and competing fixed-income opportunities, shifting supply-demand dynamics Soska recommends MSTR partially close its long by selling 30k-60k BTC and repurchasing STRC at a discount to lock in net gains, while capping future issuance, arguing this deleverages efficiently without needing unsustainable dividend hikes
author: admin#1742094092

text_x: Before It Was Obvious Most founders quit prematurely after seeing crowded markets in AI, crypto, and tech, despite believing they could match successful founders like those behind Cursor Recurring pattern in companies like Cursor, Stripe, Figma, and Shopify: identifying early secular shifts, holding non-consensus beliefs about future customer behavior, and building for power users years before markets mature Practical advice includes deeply immersing in a space as a power user, targeting "hair on fire" problems with low switching-cost MVPs, prioritizing distribution alongside product, and persisting through early rejection to compound insights

text_x: yeh, why do i care? they ask. i care because my name is important than me. i take pride in making smth without resorting to scamming or taking private deals or collecting 6-figure ref-link checks per month. for me it is not "winning at all costs". there are ways of winning where i would rather choose to be poor than going this route. and PnD in the form of shilling and dumping from hidden wallets is the pinnacle of this for me. and ofc when i get accused by that from big accounts, without any shred of proof i get mad and cannot let it stand. wise man once said: "if you lose your money, you lose something, but if you lose your character you have lost everything." i take pride in being transparent, in showing the ups and downs of this life as they come, in not building a facade for social media clout. i despise these people. so ofc its important to me.

text_x: fucking lowlife scum. they drag you through the mud and clown you and make fun of you and accuse you of this and that without ever shown anything themselves. no doxxed wallets, no leaderboard position, no track record of any kind. just nameless haters who never spent a minute in the arena. all they can do is comment on the work on others and spew their hate.
author: admin#2325165654
text_y: fucking lowlife scum. they drag you through the mud and clown you and make fun of you and accuse you of this and that without ever shown anything themselves. no doxxed wallets, no leaderboard position, no track record of any kind. just nameless haters who never spent a minute in the arena. all they can do is comment on the work on others and spew their hate.

text_x: i mean, this is all logged. not only here and on twitter, also on the wallets themselves. immutable ledger, we remember. yet people just say stuff like "you made all your money by PnDing lowcaps". how can this be when 80%+ of my winnings came from assets >50m mc? how can this be when i logged a total of 3 (!!!) sub 1m mc coins? how can this be when my 3 biggest individual winners were HYPE (450k), TRUMP (250k) and BTC (220k)? how can this be when not a single shred of evidence of me counter-trading challenge entries from hidden wallets exist?
author: admin#2325165654

text_x: i traded hundreds of assets in my trading challenges. from trillion dollar assets like BTC and QQQ, billion dollar coins like HYPE and TRUMP over on-chain midcaps in the 50m to 400m mc range to sub 10m lowcaps. i think over all challenges i traded 2 or 3 sub 1m mc coins for some small amounts. most amount of money was made in the range 50m to 400m and HYPE and TRUMP were the 2 biggest individual wins. everything said here can be looked up, its all there. in my twitter, in this tg channel and my wallets y22.eth and y22.sol. i never had a min or max mc criteria. just traded what i wanted to trade and logged my trades. this has not changed from me having 15 followers until now. most of my logged buys and sells don't even have a thesis attached. i am not "shilling coins" or raiding tweets. yet, almost every week some retard calls me a PnD scammer or compares me to Spider. when i ask why, they come up with some tweets from 2024 where i logged a trade (buy and sell) of some on-chain lowcap with sub 1m mc for a 1k buy ticket size. today it happened again. time to explain these smoothbrains how PnDs work. the goal of these people is to buy from a HIDDEN wallet, then buy from a DOXXED wallet and then sell from the HIDDEN wallet. makes zero sense to do all of this on the same wallet. fucking Albert Einstein over here. cannot just walk around accusing people of wrongdoing without any single shred of evidence. so many people just got bitter over time. anonymous haters with zero public track record making fun of others. not a day spent in the arena, only hating from the sidelines. fucking insects.
author: admin#2325165654

text_x: Microsoft Threat Intelligence and Microsoft Defender Experts said they identified a Windows-based crypto clipper that has affected users since February 2026. The malware spreads via malicious .lnk shortcuts and USB drives, launches a bundled Tor proxy through Windows Script Host and ActiveX, and connects to hidden-service C2 servers. It can steal clipboard data, exfiltrate seed phrases and private keys, capture screenshots, and replace crypto wallet addresses. Microsoft Defender Antivirus detects it as Trojan:Win32/CryptoBandits.A. — link

text_x: Former Ethereum Foundation contributor Trent VanEpps said Ethereum could face a "slow-burning funding crisis" for core development within the next 3–9 months, citing the Ethereum Foundation's spending reductions and the expiration of the Client Incentive Program (CIP). He estimated Ethereum's core development ecosystem requires roughly $30 million in annual funding and argued that new institutions and funding mechanisms will be needed as the Ethereum Foundation was not designed to be a permanent steward of the network. — link
